vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a Jatco CVT — shift lock slot location: Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. AWD available on newer gen.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (newer).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (2022+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (2022+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD/2WD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) on ground. 4WD/AWD: transfer case to 2WD or flatbed. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: 4th gen (2022+) Pathfinder: FWD or AWD — FWD: rear lift OK. AWD: flatbed only.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid: 12V only.

Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.

Move the selector to Neutral.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
CVT transmissions: do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with driven wheels on ground — flatbed strongly preferred.

towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base (older) or electronic selector slot (2022+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap at side of console shifter base (older) or electronic selector slot (2022+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever (1st/2nd gen) or electric push-button (3rd gen 2022+) — 2H / 4H / N / 4L
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position on pavement
steps: MANUAL: Stop completely. Shift TC floor lever firmly to 2H.
ELECTRIC (2022+ 3rd gen): Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H button. Confirm indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed in 2H.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD (if equipped) — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Press the 2WD button on the center console.
Confirm 2WD indicator before lifting rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Confirm indicator before lifting rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Nissan Leaf to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — FWD Electric
knownIssues.detail: The Leaf electric motor drives the front wheels. Towing with front wheels on the ground will damage the motor. FLATBED ONLY on all Leaf models. Never wheel-lift from the front.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV traction battery. 12V auxiliary only. Flatbed only — FWD electric.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
GT-R uses a DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT) — 12V power is required to shift.
Locate the shift lock slot — Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
FLATBED ONLY — ATTESA E-TS AWD cannot be disabled.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Open the trunk — battery is in trunk, under panel. Connect jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY — AWD system is permanent and cannot be disabled.
HIGH-VALUE VEHICLE: use padded equipment only. Never contact body panels, spoiler, or diffuser.
vehicles.notes: DCT requires power for neutral shift. AWD standard.
vehicles.warn: AWD. DCT — do not tow with wheels on ground without power.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

knownIssues.title: AWD Cannot Be Disabled — Flatbed Only
knownIssues.detail: The GT-R's ATTESA E-TS AWD system is permanent and cannot be disabled. FLATBED ONLY on all GT-Rs. Attempting to wheel-lift will immediately damage the front differential.

knownIssues.title: DCT Requires Power to Shift
knownIssues.detail: The GT-R's dual-clutch transmission requires 12V power to shift to neutral. The battery must be jumped first. Battery is located under the hood.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Valuable — Handle With Care
knownIssues.detail: GT-Rs range from $60,000-$200,000+ in value. Any contact with the body panels, spoiler, or diffuser during towing can result in extremely costly damage claims. Use only designated tow points and padded equipment.

battery.jumpTerminals: In trunk — direct battery access under cargo panel
battery.warnings: AWD — flatbed only. DCT requires power before neutral shift. Very valuable vehicle — use padded equipment only.
